title: parathelypteris pectiniformis
intro: Parathelypteris pectiniformis, also known as the forked polypody, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the division Pteridophyta and is native to East Asia, particularly in China, Japan, and Korea. The forked polypody is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to relieve fever, soothe digestive issues, and treat respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Parathelypteris pectiniformis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to support the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
